subject: Chemistry
title: Thallium: The Poisoner's Poison
description: Learn Thallium: The Poisoner's Poison in one minute.

hook.text: Thallium is odorless, tasteless, and dissolves in water. It was the perfect murder weapon—used in over 30 known poisoning cases.

buildup.text: Thallium ions mimic potassium and enter cells through potassium channels. Once inside, they disrupt enzyme function.

discovery.text: Symptoms appear days later: hair loss, nerve pain, organ failure. Victims often didn't know they'd been poisoned.

twist.text: Saddam Hussein used thallium to poison political enemies. Graham Young, a British serial killer, used it to murder coworkers.

climax.text: Prussian blue is the antidote—it traps thallium ions in the gut and prevents absorption. An old pigment saves modern lives.

punchline.text: Tasteless, delayed, devastating. The element that mimics potassium to kill.

quiz.question: How does thallium poison the body?
options: It mimics potassium and enters cells through potassium channels, disrupting enzymes
It dissolves bone tissue like an acid
It blocks oxygen transport in red blood cells
